source: Bank of Zambia (boz.zm), XE.com, exchangerate.guru, valutafx
baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↓ ZMW = Kwacha strengthening (appreciating).
mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held at 13.5% at May review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: BOZ official mid-rate: 17.7050. XE.com mid-market: 17.8145 at 16:32 UTC Jun 9. 2026 low was 17.512 on Jun 4 (valutafx). XE 7-day range: 17.56–18.43. Slight bounce off multi-year lows — kwacha paused its rally.

GBP.note: XE.com mid-market: 1 GBP = 23.8315 ZMW at 16:32 UTC. GBP/ZMW cross via USD.

EUR.note: XE.com mid-market: 1 EUR = 20.5696 ZMW at 16:32 UTC. 2026 high was 26.069 on Jan 1 (valutafx). -21.1% YTD.

ZAR.note: XE.com mid-market: 1 ZAR = 1.0793 ZMW at 16:32 UTC. 1 ZMW = 0.9265 ZAR.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. Zambia's growing yuan-denominated mining tax corridor.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha PAUSED near 2-year highs — slight retreat today
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: BOZ held MPR at 13.5% at May review. Inflation at 6.8% (April). Further kwacha appreciation possible on copper tailwinds, but BOZ may intervene to protect export competitiveness. Key risks: global copper price reversal, drought cycles, election-related fiscal expansion (Aug 2026).
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Bias toward further appreciation if copper holds above $9,500/tonne and IMF successor programme progresses.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Wider range reflects election uncertainty (Aug 2026). Post-election policy continuity would support kwacha; fiscal slippage would reverse gains.
